title: Campus Housing Issue
transcript: Student: Hi, I'm calling about my housing application for next semester. I submitted it last month, but I haven't received any confirmation. Housing Officer: Let me look that up. Can I have your student ID? Student: It's 884-2910. Housing Officer: I see the issue. Your application was received, but it was flagged because you didn't indicate a roommate preference. Student: Oh, I didn't realize that was required. I don't have a specific roommate in mind. Housing Officer: That's fine. We can assign you to a random roommate or a single room if any are available. Single rooms cost an extra eight hundred dollars per semester. Student: I'll stick with a random roommate. How will I find out my assignment? Housing Officer: Room assignments will be emailed by July 15th. You'll also get your roommate's contact information so you can coordinate what to bring. Student: Great. Is there anything else I need to do? Housing Officer: Just make sure your deposit is paid by June 1st. Otherwise, your application will be cancelled. Student: I'll check my account right now. Thanks for your help.

questions.stem: Why does the student contact the housing office?
options: To ask about the status of a housing application
questions.explanation: The student has not received confirmation about a housing application submitted last month and wants to know its status.

questions.stem: What does the housing officer say about single rooms?
options: They are guaranteed for all applicants
They are only for graduate students
They are not available next semester
questions.explanation: The officer mentions that single rooms cost an extra eight hundred dollars per semester.
